issues: Minor: _release_lock() only catches FileNotFoundError, not broader OSError (Windows anti-virus could cause PermissionError)
Minor: _acquire_lock() has theoretical TOCTOU race (acceptable at 1-minute scheduling interval)
Batch files don't redirect stdout/stderr to a log file
recommendations: Consider broader except OSError in _release_lock() for Windows
Consider adding >> batch_output.log 2>&1 to batch files
Consider adding explicit --scheduled CLI flag instead of inferring from not --watch
result.summary: 95/100. All 3 output channels correctly implemented. Lock file mechanism solid with PID+timestamp, stale detection, corrupt file handling. Batch files correct. Watch mode untouched. All tests pass. Production-ready.
